subtitle: Digital Agency & Consultancy Delivery
hero_sub: 15+ years owning client relationships, project budgets, and delivery outcomes across digital agencies and consultancies — from production roots at @radical.media and SapientNitro through AI-native delivery work today. Three practices, one person: strategy, delivery, and hands-on engineering, applied to every client engagement.

the_case.need: 5+ years project management experience, ideally digital agency or consultancy
the_case.answer: 15+ years of client-facing delivery leadership — with genuine agency production roots at @radical.media, SapientNitro, and Jack Morton Worldwide, layered under a decade of consulting engagements.
the_case.detail: This is the deepest, most authentic match in my background — agency and consultancy delivery isn't an adjacent skill I'm stretching toward, it's where my career started and what it returned to.

the_case.need: Direct experience managing project budgets, timelines, and client relationships
the_case.answer: Owned client relationships and delivery budgets directly at Eptura (Operations Board), Understood.org (VP of Engineering), and Crocs (retailer leadership) — and as OVRSTCH's founder, owned budget, hiring, and revenue myself.
the_case.detail: At OVRSTCH there was no one else to hand the budget or client relationship to — I owned P&L, resourcing, and every client-facing decision as the company's sole principal.

the_case.need: Track record delivering complex web/digital projects on time and within scope
the_case.answer: Understood.org: complex Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform delivered on time, zero critical regressions. Crocs: release governance for a publicly traded retailer. OVRSTCH: full commerce platform shipped end to end.
the_case.detail: Each of these required holding scope and timeline discipline under real pressure — a distressed program in its final months at Understood.org, live audit review at Crocs, and full personal accountability at OVRSTCH.

the_case.answer: Titles have run Producer, Delivery Consultant, and Scrum Master rather than 'Product Manager' — but the scope has been the same product-thinking work throughout a decade of agency Producer roles and, most directly, as OVRSTCH's founder.
the_case.detail: At OVRSTCH I owned every product decision myself — what to build, how to price it, what to cut. The label differs; the discipline is the same one this role needs.

the_case.need: Hands-on AI tools experience and interest in cutting-edge technology
the_case.answer: Daily Claude Code practitioner since 2022. Shipped 6 production applications in 2026. Embedded Jira AI and Copilot into client delivery workflows at Crocs, cutting administrative overhead ~30%.
the_case.detail: This is applied intelligence, not strategy-deck interest — I use these tools every day to build things and to make client delivery faster.

the_case.need: Team hiring and development; contribute to hiring and onboarding (bonus: agency/consultancy)
the_case.answer: Recruited and onboarded contract talent as OVRSTCH's founder with no HR department. Earlier career team-building at Bushwick Art Project / BAPLAB.
the_case.detail: At OVRSTCH, every hire was mine to make and get productive fast — no recruiter, no HR team, just judgment calls under real constraints.

the_case.answer: BD support across most of my agency career, plus self-driven business development at OVRSTCH and a stealth-mode AI startup — scoping, pricing, and securing revenue directly.
the_case.detail: As OVRSTCH's sole principal, business development wasn't a separate function I could hand off — I identified and closed new revenue myself.

the_stack.headline: Four practices, one project lead.
the_stack.subhead: Frontier technology. Applied intelligence. Human-centered delivery.

nodes.label: Strategy
nodes.desc: Client relationships and product thinking, focused on real user and business outcomes
nodes.pm_role: Eptura Operations Board · Understood.org VP Eng · Crocs retailer leadership

nodes.label: Delivery
nodes.desc: Budgets, timelines, and cross-functional teams — design, engineering, and strategy — delivered on scope
nodes.pm_role: ~10 distributed teams at Eptura · 12+ countries at Understood.org · live audit review at Crocs

nodes.label: Engineering
nodes.desc: AI-assisted building — internal tools, prototypes, and workflow automations, built hands-on
nodes.pm_role: 6 production apps shipped in 2026 · Jira AI & Copilot embedded at Crocs

nodes.label: Growth
nodes.desc: Team hiring and business development, owned personally where there was no one else to hand it to
nodes.pm_role: OVRSTCH hiring & BD · Bushwick Art Project / BAPLAB · agency-era BD support

when_things_break.scenario: A compliance requirement threatens to slow delivery to a crawl
response: At Crocs, designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls
Embedded them directly into the sprint workflow — not a separate compliance gate, but part of the definition of done
Kept the team fast while the audit trail stayed clean
That's the seam a good project lead owns, not a blocker they wait for legal to clear

when_things_break.scenario: A founder needs to build and ship a product with no team to delegate to
response: At OVRSTCH, I was the only person
Recruited contract talent, drove business development myself, and shipped a full commerce platform end to end
Owned every product, budget, and hiring decision personally
There's no substitute for having actually run the whole thing yourself before asking anyone else to

when_things_break.scenario: A distributed program is in crisis and needs stabilization fast
response: At Understood.org, inherited a distressed, 12+-country program in its final crucial months
Established daily executive syncs and a visible recovery plan immediately
Rebuilt reporting transparency so the next update was never a surprise
Delivered on time with zero critical regressions

how_i_run_a_room: Every client relationship gets a proactive status cadence — kickoffs, status meetings, retrospectives — before the client has to ask
Budgets and change orders are tracked continuously, not reconstructed when a client questions an invoice
Product thinking shows up in every engagement: what outcome does this actually need to produce, not just what was scoped
AI tools get used daily to speed up delivery — documentation, planning, and prototyping — with human judgment always in control
Team members get hired and onboarded fast, with clear ownership from day one
Attention to detail is a standard I hold myself to first, before I ask a team to raise theirs

points: Founded and delivered an AI-native commerce platform solo, concept to production
Recruited, onboarded, and managed contract talent with no HR department
Drove business development directly — scoping, pricing, securing revenue
Diagnosed and resolved a production bottleneck, cutting cycle time ~25%

track_record.role: Project Delivery Consultant / Release Governance Lead
points: Owned the client relationship and release governance cadence for a publicly traded retailer
Designed SOX-compliant Segregation of Duties controls embedded in sprint workflow
Built 'Release Radar,' a real-time delivery-readiness tracking framework
Reduced administrative overhead ~30% via Jira AI and Copilot
Owned delivery coordination across ~10 distributed teams spanning India and California
Produced executive dashboards tracking delivery KPIs for senior leadership
Drove 35%+ increase in delivery efficiency, 20% reduction in sprint carryover
Coached teams and Product Owners across Marketing, UX, Engineering, and Data
Stabilized a distressed, multi-year program spanning 12+ countries
Steered a Sitecore-to-Contentful replatform to on-time launch
Primary point of contact for the VP of Engineering and executive stakeholders
Delivered through QA/UAT with zero critical release regressions

before: Client relationships feel transactional — status reports instead of partnership
Budgets and change orders get reconciled after the fact, not tracked continuously
AI tools are a strategy slide, not something the team actually uses daily
Product thinking gets lost in scope compliance
after: Client relationships become partnerships built on proactive communication
Budgets and change orders are visible in real time, before they become surprises
AI tools are embedded in daily delivery work, speeding up documentation and planning
Every engagement centers on the real user and business outcome, not just the scope document

the_ramp.deliverable: Review the current client portfolio, project budgets, and team structure; sit in on a client status meeting to understand the relationship dynamics already in play.
Own day-to-day delivery for at least one active client engagement; identify the first opportunity to embed an AI tool into the team's workflow.

Own full client relationship and budget responsibility for an assigned engagement; contribute to a proposal or scoping conversation; deliver the first process improvement recommendation for the PM team.
